segments.text: Then Bildad the Shuhite spoke up and said,
How long will you go on talking, hunting for the right words to say? Talk sense if you want us to reply!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Do you think we're dumb animals? Do we look stupid to you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You tear yourself apart with your anger.  Do you think the earth has to be abandoned, or the mountains moved, just because of you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: It's certain that the life of the wicked will end like a lamp that is snuffed out—their flame will shine no more.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The light in their home goes out, the lamp hanging above is extinguished.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Instead of taking strong strides they stumble, and their own plans cause them to fall.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Their own feet trip them up and they are caught in a net; as they walk along they fall into a pit.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: A trap grabs them by the heel; a snare tightens around them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: A noose is hidden on the ground for them; a rope is stretched across the path to trip them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Terrors scare the wicked, coming at them from every side, chasing them, biting at their heels.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Hunger robs them of strength; disaster waits for them when they fall.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Disease devours their skin; deadly disease consumes their limbs.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are torn from the homes they trusted in and taken to the king of terrors.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: People they don't know will live in their homes; sulfur will be scattered where they used to live.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They wither away, roots below and branches above;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: the memory of them fades from the earth; nobody remembers their names any more.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are thrown out of light into darkness, driven from the world.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They have no children or descendants among their people, and no survivors where they used to live.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: People of the west are appalled at what happens to them. People of the east are shocked.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This is what happens to the homes of the wicked, to the places of those who reject God.
segments.speaker: Unknown
